params.name: Guru Granth Sahib
params.description: The holiest scripture of Sikhism, revered as a living Guru beyond the written word. It spans 1,430 pages in Gurmukhi script, compiling 5,894 hymns from 33 spiritual voices—including Sikh Gurus, Bhakti poets, and Sufi saints. Housed in temples under silk, it’s fanned day and night, read with ritual purity; desecration is cause for armed defense.
